title: Today's AI Brief: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
summary: A look at the value of Anthropic stock in San Francisco real estate and AI in healthcare
imageAlt: Image from source article for What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
imageCaption: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
imageSourceTitle: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. Let's dive into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. I've got a couple of stories that caught my eye recently, and I think you'll find them pretty fascinating.

So, it turns out that in San Francisco, Anthropic stock is worth more than cash in some real estate deals. I mean, we all know that the median house price in San Francisco is now over $2 million, but it's crazy to think that Anthropic stock is being used as part of the payment. According to a recent article in Wired, at least seven houses in the city sold for $1 million over the asking price, with Anthropic stock being used to seal the deal. This just highlights the value of AI companies in the Bay Area, where cash can be scarce. It's like the stock is the new currency.

Boston Children's Hospital is using OpenAI technology to improve patient care and diagnose rare diseases. They've had success with over 40 cases, which is a great example of AI being used to make a positive impact on people's lives. It's amazing to think that AI can help doctors unlock new diagnoses and provide better care for patients. I love seeing AI being used in practical ways like this.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
